nodes.name: OpenAI
nodes.summary: OpenAI is a company that develops and releases AI models, including GPT-5.

nodes.name: GPT-5
nodes.summary: GPT-5 is an AI model developed by OpenAI that requires a monthly enterprise license for API access.

nodes.name: Meta
nodes.summary: Meta is a company that releases open-source models, including those that benchmark within 8% of GPT-4 performance.

nodes.name: GPT-4
nodes.summary: GPT-4 is an AI model that serves as a benchmark for other models, including those released by Meta.

nodes.name: Stanford
nodes.summary: Stanford is an institution where researchers have published findings on the impact of new pricing models for AI access.

nodes.name: Large corporations
nodes.summary: Large corporations are customers that will have access to advanced AI capabilities under the new pricing model.

nodes.name: Government agencies
nodes.summary: Government agencies are organizations that will have access to advanced AI capabilities under the new pricing model.

nodes.name: Small startups
nodes.summary: Small startups are organizations that will be effectively priced out of cutting-edge AI access under the new pricing model.

nodes.name: Academic researchers
nodes.summary: Academic researchers are individuals who will be affected by the new pricing model for AI access.
